latest.title: Concert Party Update
latest.content:
Today 10:00
RNS Number : 8733V
Crimson Tide PLC
09 March 2026
9 March 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Confirmation of changes to the concert party
Crimson Tide, the provider of the mpro5 process management app, announces recent changes to the composition of the concert party in respect of the Company.
Under the City Code on Takeovers and Mergers (the "
Code
") published by the Panel on Takeovers and Mergers (the "
Panel
"), a concert party arises where persons who, pursuant to an agreement or understanding (whether formal or informal), co-operate to obtain or consolidate control (as defined in the Code) of a company or to frustrate the successful outcome of an offer for a company.
Certain persons will be presumed, as set out in the Definitions in the Code, to be persons acting in concert with other persons in the same category unless the contrary is established, including shareholders in a private company who sell their shares or interests in consideration for the issue of new shares in a company to which the Code applies, or who, in connection with an initial public offering or otherwise, become shareholders in a company to which the Code applies.
At the time of its admission to AIM on 22 August 2006, the Company stated in its AIM Admission Document that it had agreed with the Panel that a concert party existed in the context of the Company (the "
IPO Concert Party
") which held approximately 76% of the Company's issued share capital. M
aterial developments since that time have prompted a review of the IPO concert party, including a share consolidation; the deaths of several IPO Concert Party members; and the disassociation of certain IPO Concert Party members.
Accordingly, the Company has now agreed with the Panel that the IPO Concert Party is now comprised of the following members, who together hold approximately 19.96% of the Company's issued share capital:
Shareholder
No. shares
%
Barrie and Helen Whipp
628,102

19.96%
Under Rule 9 of the Code, any person who acquires an interest in shares which, taken together with shares in which that person or any person acting in concert with that person is interested, carry 30% or more of the voting rights of a company which is subject to the Code is normally required to make an offer to all the remaining shareholders to acquire their shares.

latest.title: Non-Executive Director Appointments
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4441U
Crimson Tide PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Non-Executive Director appointments
The Board of Crimson Tide
plc (AIM: TIDE),
the provider of the mpro5 process management
platform
, is delighted to announce the appointments of Nicky Chenery and Ira Roxburgh as Non-Executive Directors of the Company.
Nicky has over 20 years' experience building go-to-market functions and scaling SaaS businesses. Most recently, Nicky has held senior leadership roles across a number of B2B organisations, including Wix, DeepL and SafetyCulture, following earlier experience at Adobe and Yell.
Ira has over 18 years' experience in product leadership, with a strong emphasis on global product development, scaling products and maximising capital efficiency across both consumer and B2B sectors. Most recently, Ira has served as Chief Product Officer at Checkatrade and before that, Zettle by PayPal, following earlier senior product roles at Funding Circle, Expedia, Amazon and Microsoft.
Nicky and Ira will join the Company's Remuneration Committee and Audit Committee, each of which is chaired by James Joyce, Non-Executive Director of the Company.
Chris Fielding, Non-Executive Chair of Crimson Tide, commented:
"We are delighted to announce the appointments of Nicky and Ira and look forward with excitement to the contributions that both will undoubtedly make to our developing business."
Jon Clarke, Chief Executive Officer of Crimson Tide, commented:
"We have ambitious growth plans for the business and have carefully considered where we can further strengthen the Board to support those ambitions. The two key areas identified were product and go-to-market execution. Ira brings significant experience in defining and executing product strategy, having led product teams for some of the most widely used platforms globally.
Nicky has a strong track record of building high-performing commercial teams and delivering sustained revenue growth. Nicky's experience at SafetyCulture, a business operating within our space, will be particularly valuable, having scaled the EMEA region from an early stage through an effective land-and-expand strategy aligned with our own. I am delighted to welcome both appointments to the Board and believe they will play an important role in supporting the Company's growth strategy."
Further disclosures pursuant to the AIM Rules for Companies:
Nicky Chenery's full married name is Nicola Suzanne Gardiner (nee Chenery). Nicky is aged 50 and has not held any directorships or partnerships in the past five years.
Ira Roxburgh's full married name is Irina Roxburgh (nee Ignatova). Ira is aged 53, is currently a director of Fieldlimit Limited and has not held any other directorships or partnerships in the past five years.

latest.title: Renewal of Contract
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3867T
Crimson Tide PLC
18 February 2026
REACH: non-regulatory announcement*
18 February 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Renewal of Contract
Crimson Tide plc (AIM: TIDE), the provider of the mpro5 operational compliance platform, is pleased to announce the renewal of a 36‑month contract with Koenig & Bauer (UK) Ltd ("
Koenig & Bauer
"), part of Koenig & Bauer AG, one of the world's oldest and most established printing press manufacturers.
Koenig & Bauer has been a valued customer of Crimson Tide since the early years of the Company's commercial operations. Over this time, they have adopted mpro5 in a highly specialised and unique way, integrating the platform deeply into their service and operational workflows. Their renewed commitment reflects the long‑standing partnership between the two companies and the continued relevance of mpro5 in supporting Koenig & Bauer's operational excellence.
Commenting on the renewal, Jon Clarke, CEO of Crimson Tide plc, said:
"We are delighted that Koenig & Bauer has chosen to renew their contract with us for a further 36 months. As one of our earliest adopters, they exemplify the long-term value that mpro5 can deliver, particularly when used in innovative and specialised ways. This renewal reinforces the strength of our long-standing relationship and supports our focus on sustainable, recurring growth."
Enquiries:

latest.title: Change of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0459Q
Crimson Tide PLC
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Change of Non-Executive Director
Crimson Tide, the provider of the mpro5 process management app, announces that Janet Morris has tendered her resignation as a Non-Executive Director of the Company, which will take effect immediately.
The Company had already initiated a process to bring new non-executive skills to its Board and will, accordingly, accelerate that process and make a further announcement as soon as practicable.
Chris Fielding, Chair of the Company, commented: "The Board would like to place on record its thanks for Janet's valuable contribution to the Company and wishes her every success in her future endeavours".

company_30s.what_they_do: Crimson Tide Plc designs and delivers software that connects health‑care providers to patient records, billing systems and NHS digital platforms.  Its core product suite helps hospitals and GP practices share data securely and streamline administrative workflows.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK NHS is moving rapidly toward a fully digital ecosystem.  With billions of pounds earmarked for IT upgrades, a company that can plug into existing systems and offer compliance‑ready solutions has a clear market advantage.  Crimson Tide’s tools reduce manual entry, cut errors and cut costs for providers, making it a valuable partner in a sector that is forced to modernise.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently renewed a key NHS contract and appointed a new CEO, signalling a shift from a cash‑burn phase toward a more stable, revenue‑generating operation.  It is still small and its share price is low, but the latest announcements suggest it is moving from a survival mode into a growth‑ready phase.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy shuffling its board – a new chief executive, a new finance director and a new chair – all aimed at tightening governance and positioning for the next funding round.
A February renewal of a long‑term NHS contract confirms that the core business model is working and provides a predictable cash stream for the next 12 months.
Recent shareholder activity shows institutional investors adding positions, hinting that smart money believes in the turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is listening for the contract renewal to ignite a bounce.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the new leadership and the NHS deal as a recipe for a clean recovery and are quietly building positions.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices worry that the company’s thin capital base and limited liquidity could expose it to volatility, especially if the new contract doesn’t deliver expected revenue.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for months but is now slightly positive as the February renewal announcement approaches.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which means a news event can create a surprise spike.

near_term_catalysts: The February 18, 2026 contract renewal announcement – a 12‑month NHS deal worth £2.5 million – is the most obvious trigger for a price move.
The upcoming AGM results on October 31, 2025 could confirm that the new contract is delivering cash and may unlock further share liquidity.
A first quarterly earnings release from the new CEO’s tenure would provide a clear gauge of revenue momentum.
A spike in trading volume above 10 k shares on a single day would confirm that the market is beginning to price in the contract.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 3‑day breakout above the 0.02 p support level, accompanied by a volume surge of at least 20 % over the 5‑day average.  A positive earnings surprise or a new NHS tender win would also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stages of a post‑capitulation rebound.  The APEX score indicates a tight compression, so a breakout is likely soon if the February announcement holds up.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company’s crash depth is deep, and the new contract plus leadership overhaul provide a clear path to revenue growth.  If the price climbs from its current 0.01 p to 0.05 p – a 400 % jump – the return would be in line with the historical 162 % average for RED signals.  The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the stock is now trading well below its 52‑week high.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm remains thin‑cap and highly volatile.  If the NHS contract does not materialise as expected or if the new CEO fails to hit milestones, the price could stay stagnant or even fall further.  The worst‑case downside is a 50 % drop from the current level, still leaving the share above zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 4:1 – a 400 % gain versus a 20 % loss if the price falls to 0.008 p.  The asymmetry is clear: the upside far outweighs the limited downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 0.02 p with a stop 0.015 p to protect against a false move.  Size the position so that a 20 % loss would be acceptable – typically 10–15 % of capital per trade.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the February 18 announcement.  If volume spikes and the price closes above 0.02 p for two consecutive days, then consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks, so the setup remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash, a fresh NHS contract and new leadership, the stock offers a high‑reward chance for a sharp rebound – a classic contrarian play where the upside far eclipses the modest downside.
